createAcronymFunction.name: Create acronym function
createAcronymFunction.description: Write a function that takes a phrase and returns an acronym. The acronym should be formed by taking the first letter of each word and converting it to uppercase.

pickLetters.question: How do I pick out which letters become the acronym?
pickLetters.answer: The letters you want are the ones that start each word. That's usually the very first character of the phrase, plus any character that comes right after a space or hyphen.

findWordStarts.question: How do I find the characters that come right after a space or hyphen?
findWordStarts.answer: Loop through the phrase one character at a time, keeping track of what the previous character was. If the previous character was a space or hyphen (or you're at the very start), the current character is the start of a word.

buildResult.question: How do I build up the result as I go?
buildResult.answer: Start with an empty result string and append each chosen letter onto the end using concatenation with `+`.

lowercaseResult.question: I've got the right letters but my answer's lowercase
lowercaseResult.answer: Once you've built the full string, convert it to uppercase in one go using `toUpperCase()` before returning it.

toUpperCase.description: Convert a string to uppercase (provided by level stdlib)
